agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation, trials) with payment validation, retry logic, and tier-specific grace periods.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing & Lifecycle Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, with a defined billing cycle and tier."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect recurring charges. Has a status including 'failed'."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when charges are due."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automated process of charging the customer and extending the subscription for another billing cycle."},{"term":"Renewal Attempt","definition":"A single try to collect payment during the renewal window. Up to 3 attempts for standard tiers."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A tier-specific window after payment failure during which the subscription remains active before cancellation."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that receives a 14-day grace period instead of the standard 3-strike cancellation rule."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A time-limited subscription that allows a customer to use the service without payment, limited to one active trial per email."},{"term":"Cancelled Subscription","definition":"A terminal state. The subscription is ended and cannot be reactivated; the customer must create a new one."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription against a known-bad payment method would create a fundamentally corrupt state: an active subscription with no ability to collect revenue. The system would be lying about its own billing capability. This is not a policy preference — it is a data consistency guard that prevents the system from entering an invalid state where services are rendered with zero expectation of payment.","testable_assertion":"Given a payment method with status 'failed', when a subscription activation is attempted using that payment method, then the activation MUST be rejected and the subscription MUST NOT transition to 'active'."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy choice designed to provide a comfortable retry window. A VP could change this to 5 days or 10 days based on payment processor SLAs, retry success rate data, or customer feedback. The system would not be in a corrupt state if this value changed — it is a tunable operational parameter, not a consistency constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an active subscription, when the current date is exactly 7 days before the billing cycle end date, then the system MUST initiate an automatic renewal attempt."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The threshold of 3 attempts is a business policy decision balancing customer retention against revenue risk. Management could reasonably change this to 2 or 5 attempts based on churn analysis or payment processor recommendations. The number itself is not a data integrity constraint — it is a configurable policy threshold.","testable_assertion":"Given an active standard-tier subscription with 2 prior failed renewal attempts, when a 3rd renewal attempt fails, then the subscription MUST be automatically transitioned to 'cancelled' status."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. 'Cancelled' is a terminal state. Allowing reactivation would violate the directed acyclic nature of the subscription lifecycle, creating ambiguity around billing history, audit trails, trial eligibility, and revenue recognition. If a cancelled subscription could be reactivated, you could not trust historical records — did the customer have continuous service? Were there gaps? This is not a policy preference; it enforces the irreversibility of a terminal state, which is fundamental to data integrity and financial auditability.","testable_assertion":"Given a subscription in 'cancelled' status, when any attempt is made to transition it to 'active' status, then the transition MUST be rejected and the subscription MUST remain 'cancelled'."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 14-day window and the decision to use a time-based grace period (vs. attempt-count-based) for premium tier are business policy decisions reflecting the higher value of premium customers. A VP could change this to 21 days, or revert premium to a strike-based model with 5 attempts, without corrupting system state. This is a tier-specific policy parameter, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a premium-tier subscription whose renewal payment has failed, when 14 days have elapsed since the first failed renewal attempt without a successful payment, then the subscription MUST be automatically cancelled."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a uniqueness constraint that preserves data integrity and prevents abuse. Allowing multiple concurrent trials per email would mean the system cannot enforce trial boundaries — it creates a state where a single identity consumes multiple trial grants simultaneously, which is fundamentally incoherent. This is analogous to a unique constraint in a database: the system's data model is corrupt if two active trials exist for the same email. While the business reason is abuse prevention, the constraint itself is a hard integrity rule — the system should never be in a state where this is true.","testable_assertion":"Given an email address that already has an active free trial subscription, when a new free trial subscription is requested for the same email address, then the request MUST be rejected and no new trial subscription MUST be created."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer with a payment method marked as 'failed'","when":"They attempt to activate a new subscription","then":"The system rejects the activation and returns an error indicating the payment method must be valid"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a billing cycle ending in exactly 7 days","when":"The renewal scheduler runs","then":"A renewal attempt is initiated for that subscription"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A standard-tier subscription that has accumulated 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts","when":"The 3rd failure is recorded","then":"The subscription is automatically transitioned to 'cancelled' status"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription in 'cancelled' status","when":"Any reactivation is attempted (API call, admin action, or automated process)","then":"The system rejects the reactivation and requires creation of a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A premium-tier subscription with a failed renewal payment","when":"14 calendar days pass from the first failure without a successful payment","then":"The subscription is automatically cancelled"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A premium-tier subscription with a failed renewal within the 14-day grace period","when":"A successful payment is collected before the 14 days elapse","then":"The subscription remains active and the grace period resets"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An email address with an existing active free trial","when":"A new free trial is requested for that same email","then":"The request is rejected with a message that only one active trial per email is allowed"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An email address whose previous free trial has expired or been cancelled","when":"A new free trial is requested for that email","then":"The system evaluates the request without blocking on the prior trial (spec does not prohibit this)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Payment method fails between subscription creation and activation","expected_behavior":"If the payment method status changes to 'failed' after the subscription is created but before activation, the activation must still be blocked per BR-1."},{"scenario":"3rd renewal attempt fails for a premium-tier subscriber","expected_behavior":"The 3-strike rule does NOT apply. Premium subscribers use the 14-day grace period (BR-5). The number of attempts is irrelevant for premium; only elapsed time matters."},{"scenario":"Customer updates payment method during the renewal retry window","expected_behavior":"The system should reattempt renewal with the updated payment method. A successful charge resets the failure count and prevents cancellation."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber's payment fails on day 13, succeeds on retry on day 14","expected_behavior":"Spec says '14-day grace period before cancellation' — if payment succeeds within the 14-day window, the subscription should remain active. Boundary condition: does day 14 count as within or past the grace period? Needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Free trial subscriber changes email and requests a new trial","expected_behavior":"The constraint is per email address. A different email with no active trial would be permitted under the spec as written, though this may represent an abuse vector the business should consider."},{"scenario":"Subscription renewal scheduled 7 days out but billing cycle end date is changed","expected_behavior":"If the billing cycle is modified (e.g., proration, plan change), the renewal trigger date should recalculate to maintain the 7-day lead time."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect enforcement of BR-1 could result in active subscriptions with no collectible revenue. Miscounting renewal attempts or grace periods directly impacts churn rate and revenue recovery. Trial abuse (BR-6 violation) erodes the conversion funnel economics.","operational":"The 7-day renewal window and retry logic require reliable job scheduling infrastructure. Premium grace period tracking adds state management complexity. Trial uniqueness enforcement requires a performant lookup mechanism.","user":"Customers expect clear communication at each failure point. Premium customers expect differentiated treatment during payment issues. Trial users blocked from a second trial need clear messaging about why.","risk":"If BR-4 (terminal cancellation) is violated, audit and revenue recognition become unreliable. If BR-1 is violated, bad debt accumulates. If BR-6 is violated, trial abuse can scale rapidly and distort acquisition metrics.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (Standard Tier)","impact":"Subject to 3-strike cancellation; need clear notifications at each failure to avoid involuntary churn."},{"group":"Customers (Premium Tier)","impact":"Receive more lenient 14-day grace period; expect this as a differentiating benefit of their tier."},{"group":"Finance / Revenue Team","impact":"Depends on BR-4 for clean revenue recognition and BR-1 for collectibility assurance."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle inquiries from cancelled subscribers who want to reactivate; must enforce BR-4 and guide toward new subscription creation."},{"group":"Growth / Acquisition Team","impact":"Depends on BR-6 to ensure trial conversion metrics are not diluted by repeat trial abuse."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is reasonably clear on most rules but has notable gaps: the interaction between the 3-strike rule and the premium grace period is implicit rather than explicit (I inferred they are mutually exclusive). The boundary condition on the 14-day grace period (inclusive vs exclusive of day 14) is unspecified. Whether 'per email address' trial uniqueness applies only to concurrent trials or also prohibits repeat trials after expiration is ambiguous — I interpreted it as concurrent only based on the phrase 'at any time' qualifying 'active trial'. These gaps prevent higher confidence.","high_confidence_areas":["BR-1: Failed payment method blocks activation — clearly a state integrity rule","BR-4: Cancelled is terminal — unambiguous state machine constraint","BR-6: One active trial per email — clear uniqueness constraint","The invariant classifications are well-grounded in the distinction between data integrity and policy"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether premium tier is subject to ANY attempt limit or purely time-based","Exact boundary semantics of the 14-day grace period","Whether expired/cancelled trials for an email allow a new trial (spec says 'active' which implies yes, but business intent may differ)","What happens to in-flight renewal attempts when a subscription is manually cancelled"],"assumptions":["Premium tier grace period and standard tier 3-strike rule are mutually exclusive mechanisms","The 14-day grace period clock starts from the first failed renewal attempt","'At any time' in BR-6 means the constraint only applies to concurrently active trials, not lifetime trial limits","A successful payment during any retry window (standard or premium) restores the subscription to good standing"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not clarify whether premium subscribers are also subject to any maximum attempt count during their 14-day grace period, or whether attempts are unlimited within that window. These are meaningfully different: unlimited retries within 14 days vs. 3 retries within 14 days.","affected_stakeholders":["Premium Tier Customers","Finance / Revenue Team","Engineering"],"options":["A: Premium tier has unlimited retry attempts within the 14-day window (time-based only)","B: Premium tier has a higher attempt limit (e.g., 5-6) within the 14-day window (hybrid model)"],"recommendation":"Option A — the spec explicitly says 'instead of the 3-strike rule', suggesting the strike mechanism is replaced entirely by a time-based grace period."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says customers 'must create a new subscription' after cancellation but does not specify whether the new subscription resets trial eligibility, carries over billing history, or inherits any attributes from the cancelled subscription.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance / Revenue Team","Customer Support"],"options":["A: New subscription is fully independent — no history carried over","B: New subscription inherits certain attributes (e.g., pricing tier, payment history) from the cancelled one"],"recommendation":"Option A — a terminal state implies a clean break. But product team should confirm, especially regarding promotional pricing and loyalty benefits."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
